_meta.intake: feed-embedded interactive A2UI asks — small single-purpose surfaces rendered inline in chat messages, so ADR-0089's clarify/negotiate asks become clickable structured UI (ADR-0097)
_meta.planes: OUTSIDE-IN by module ownership (wire · sub-catalog policy · prompt · page lifecycle · docs); INSIDE-OUT derived independently from the three design problems + the smaller asks in the dispatch, then cross-mapped

nodes.label: feed-embedded interactive A2UI asks (ADR-0097)
wire channel — the ask declaration on the ADR-0088 meta envelope; ask payload rides the SAME validated stream
meta envelope `ask` extension — `A2uiMetaEnvelope.a2uiMeta.ask?: { surfaceId }` + readMetaLine shallow-validation (versionless discriminator unchanged; unknown-field drop preserved)
nodes.accept: meta-line.test.ts: readMetaLine parses {note, ask:{surfaceId}} and returns the ask; a malformed ask (non-object / missing surfaceId string) yields the envelope WITHOUT ask; a line with `version` still returns undefined; npm test green

nodes.label: produce(): peel the model-authored ask, re-compose the outgoing meta-line (note + ask + trace), ask-integrity degrade (ask declared but no payload line targets that surfaceId, or surfaceId collides with a session-known surface -> drop ask, note stands)
nodes.accept: produce-loop.test.ts: a stub emitting meta{note,ask}+createSurface/updateComponents for that id yields the meta-line FIRST with ask intact then the validated lines; a stub declaring ask with zero matching payload lines yields the meta-line with ask REMOVED; whole payload still passes the shared validateA2ui before any line streams; npm test green

nodes.label: produce(): feed-scope gate — AFTER the shared validator, every component type on the ask-routed surface must be in FEED_SURFACE_TYPES; violation feeds back a correction round (trace failureCodes carries 'FEED_SCOPE'); SPEC-R9/the shared validator untouched
nodes.accept: produce-loop.test.ts: a stub whose ask surface uses Modal gets a feedback round naming FEED_SCOPE + the offending type and succeeds on the corrected retry; the shared validateA2ui call sites are unchanged in the diff (no fork, SPEC-N3); npm test green

nodes.label: recorded transport parity — RecordedTurn.ask?: { surfaceId }; createRecordedTransport composes the same meta-line shape produce() emits (transcript NOT seeded — ADR-0089's open backbone fork stands)
nodes.accept: round-trip.test.ts (extended): a transcript turn carrying ask streams {a2uiMeta:{note,ask}} ahead of byte-identical lines; the shipped transcript is UNCHANGED (no scripted ask seeded); npm test green

nodes.label: feed sub-catalog policy — a gate-encoded total partition of catalog.json types (the ADR-0087 lesson)
feed-catalog artifact — tools/agent/feed-catalog.ts: FEED_SURFACE_TYPES (23) + FEED_EXCLUDED (11, each with a recorded reason); pure, zero-dep
nodes.accept: the module exports the two sets; every FEED_EXCLUDED entry carries a non-empty reason string; no import besides types; npm run check green

nodes.label: partition gate test — src/live-agent/feed-catalog.test.ts: IN ∪ OUT = Object.keys(catalog.components) exactly, disjoint; composite closure (parent in <=> child in) for RadioGroup/Radio, SegmentedControl/Segment, Select+ComboBox/Option, Card/CardHeader+CardContent+CardFooter, Tabs/Tab+TabPanel, Menu/MenuItem; negative control proves a planted undispositioned type FAILS
nodes.accept: npm test green; commenting one catalog type out of both sets turns the gate red (negative control asserted in-test via a synthetic catalog); a new catalog.json type with no disposition fails CI

nodes.label: derived feed inventory — the grammar mechanics block composes its allowed-types list FROM FEED_SURFACE_TYPES at composition time (drift impossible by construction)
nodes.accept: system-prompt-grammar.test.ts: the composed prompt's feed-allowed list SET-EQUALS FEED_SURFACE_TYPES; none of the feed prose leaks into the derived '## Available components' inventory; prompt-drift.test.ts stays green

nodes.label: prompt teaching — ask mechanics (invariant spine) + archetype vocabulary (mode-scaled block); NOT mini-skills (selection is user-intent-vocabulary-matched; an ask triggers on the AGENT's situation)

invariant mechanics block — how to emit a feed ask: the meta `ask` field, a fresh `ask-<n>` surfaceId, sendDataModel:true, exactly one commit Button (wantResponse omitted), payload = the ask surface ONLY, and the note-standalone rule (the note must carry the full question in prose — the degrade path)
nodes.accept: system-prompt-grammar.test.ts: the mechanics block is present in ALL modes (default/specific/blue-sky) verbatim-identical; grammarFor(undefined) === the literal GRAMMAR constant still holds; npm test green

nodes.label: mode-scaled archetype vocabulary — default: structured ask when options form a small closed set, prose note otherwise; specific: asks stay rare, decline-and-redirect becomes a single-choice ask; blue-sky: prefer structured asks whenever options are enumerable, option cards for negotiation, multi-round welcome. Five archetypes: closed single-choice (recommended-first), multi-select, typed-value (typed TextField/Calendar/Slider/SliderMulti + sendDataModel), boundary-negotiation option cards (Row(wrap) of Card+Button), confirm/cancel
nodes.accept: system-prompt-grammar.test.ts: each mode variant carries its dialed ask-vocabulary prose; the honesty floor is verbatim in every mode; default composition === GRAMMAR constant; npm test green

nodes.label: grammar gates extension — the standing assertions for nP1/nP2/nC3 (mechanics in every mode, feed list set-equal, no inventory leak, default byte-identity to the literal constant)
nodes.accept: npm test green with the new describe blocks; a planted feed type absent from the composed prompt fails (negative control)

nodes.label: page feed lifecycle — per-ask renderer hosts inline in chat bubbles; pending -> frozen(answered|bypassed); the browser owns ask lifecycle

ask registry + per-ask host + line routing — on meta.ask: mount a createRenderer() host into the turn's message bubble; route each A2UI line by parsed surfaceId (ask id -> ask host, else canvas host); collision guard (ask id already known to canvas -> drop ask); zero-ask-line degrade (drop bubble, note stands); client fail-closed feed check (an out-of-scope type on an ask line drops the WHOLE ask to the note, never a partial render); ask lines still enter allLines/the JSON tab (shown = produced)
nodes.accept: a site vitest (jsdom) drives a stubbed transport turn carrying meta.ask + ask lines: the ask renders inside the message bubble, canvas lines still reach the canvas host, an out-of-scope ask payload renders NO ask controls and the note text stands; check:site green

nodes.label: freeze machinery — bubble-level `inert` + data-state='answered'|'bypassed' + an annotation line; freeze-on-turn-dispatch (any next TurnInput freezes every pending ask not being answered by it => at most ONE pending ask ever exists); silent applies (wantResponse:false) do NOT freeze; lines targeting a frozen ask surfaceId are dropped + counted
nodes.accept: site vitest: after an ask action the bubble is inert with data-state=answered; after a typed prose send the pending ask flips to bypassed; a wantResponse:false canvas action leaves it pending; a later line targeting the frozen id does not mutate the frozen DOM

nodes.label: answer round-trip wiring — each ask host's onClientMessage feeds the SAME handleClientMessage (shouldRunTurn -> nextTurn); the action carries name+context+dataModel (sendDataModel) so typed values ride the existing arm; freeze precedes runTurn; the chat gains the existing describeClientMessage user entry
nodes.accept: site vitest: clicking the ask's commit button freezes the ask, adds the user chat entry, and dispatches a turn whose framed content (frameClientMessage) carries the ask surface's data model values

nodes.label: reset + halt handling — Reset disposes every ask host + clears the registry (alongside the existing canvas dispose); a ProduceHalt/transport error leaves a pending ask pending (still answerable)
nodes.accept: site vitest: after Reset the chat log holds no ask bubbles and every ask host is disposed (no listener leak — the surface count returns to zero); an error turn leaves the prior pending ask interactive

nodes.label: doc repairs (on ratification + build, per the ADR's Repairs field)

SPEC repairs — a2ui-live-agent.spec.md: NEW SPEC-R14 (feed-embedded ask surfaces: wire + lifecycle + degrade) + NEW SPEC-R15 (the feed sub-catalog partition gate); SPEC-R5 (meta envelope gains ask), SPEC-R6 (mechanics + archetypes + derived feed inventory; AC3's 'pre-ADR-0090 byte-for-byte' wording re-based to 'the literal GRAMMAR constant'), SPEC-R8 (the structured answer arm + freeze semantics), §2 Definitions + §5 typed contracts
nodes.accept: python ~/.claude/skills/prd-author/scripts/trace_check.py over the family passes; harness_checks.py spec green; every new requirement carries a PRD-G trace + testable ACs

nodes.label: LLD repairs — a2ui-live-agent.lld.md: LLD-C2 (RecordedTurn.ask), LLD-C3 (produce ask peel/compose + feed gate), LLD-C4 (mechanics/archetype composition), LLD-C9 (page registry/routing/freeze/reset), NEW LLD-C14 (feed-catalog artifact + partition gate), §4 state-machine ask arc, §6 error rows (ask-integrity degrade, feed-scope, frozen-target drop, collision)
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py lld green; every new component maps to SPEC-R14/R15; the §6 table enumerates the four new edge rows

actions.label: declare a turn's ask target on the wire without changing any transport signature or the meta discriminator
validate the ask payload with the shared validator before any line streams (parity, validate-then-stream)
restrict what a feed surface may host to a bounded, single-purpose component set
make the feed allowlist a CI-visible artifact that forces a disposition for every future catalog type
teach the model the ask mechanics and the concrete ask archetypes
scale the ask vocabulary per GenUiMode without touching the honesty floor
render an ask inline inside its own chat message, beside (not instead of) the canvas
return the user's structured answer — including typed values — as the next turn via the existing client-message arm
freeze an answered ask so history stays truthful (disabled, answer visible)
freeze a pending ask when the user answers in prose or the conversation moves on
dispose all ask state on session reset
degrade gracefully everywhere — a non-feed-aware consumer, a broken ask declaration, or an empty ask payload always falls back to the prose note
reject an out-of-scope feed payload producer-side as a self-correct round, never a stream
fail closed client-side on an out-of-scope feed line (defense in depth, no partial ask)
keep the SPEC/LLD truthful in the same change wave (repairs land with the build)
keep the recorded and live transports' stream shapes identical (the backbone CAN carry an ask)
gate the prompt's feed prose mechanically (mechanics present per mode, default byte-identity, negative control)
derive the prompt's feed-allowed list from the artifact so it cannot drift

edges.why: produce() peels/re-composes the envelope nW1 types and guards
the recorded transport serializes the same envelope shape nW1 defines
the page reads meta.ask through the agent-runtime shim's re-export of nW1's type/guard
the producer feed gate checks types against the FEED_SURFACE_TYPES set nC1 exports
the partition gate asserts the artifact's two sets against catalog.json
the derived feed inventory composes from the artifact's set at prompt build
the page's fail-closed check consumes the same set (one source, no re-spelled copy)
the mechanics block embeds the derived feed-allowed list nC3 renders
the mode-scaled vocabulary cites the mechanics block's rules; the spine must exist before the variants reference it
the gates assert the composed mechanics prose
the gates assert each mode variant's ask vocabulary
freeze operates on the registry entries + hosts nF1 creates
answer wiring subscribes to each ask host nF1 mounts
reset disposes the hosts + registry nF1 owns
the SPEC repair states the realized wire behavior (ask peel/compose + integrity degrade) by ID
SPEC-R15's ACs cite the landed partition gate's assertions
SPEC-R8's structured-answer arm cites the landed round-trip wiring
SPEC-R6's new ACs cite the landed grammar gates
LLD components trace to the SPEC requirement IDs the SPEC repair mints (R14/R15)
